---
title: "TensorToPoint2D"
---

## Overview

`TensorToPoint2D` decodes peak locations from a spatial model output channel into `Point2D<float>`
coordinates. It supports single-peak argmax decoding and multi-peak local-maximum detection.

## Tensor Layout

The decoder expects a **4D** tensor in **NCHW** order:

| Dim | Name    | Indexed by              |
|-----|---------|-------------------------|
| 0   | Batch   | `DecoderContext::batch_index` |
| 1   | Channel | `DecoderContext::source_channel` |
| 2   | Height  | `DecoderContext::height` |
| 3   | Width   | `DecoderContext::width` |

`DecoderContext::height` and `DecoderContext::width` must match `tensor.size(2)` and
`tensor.size(3)` respectively.

## Decode Modes

- `decode()` — returns the global argmax location, optionally refined with parabolic subpixel
  fitting. Returns `{0, 0}` when the selected channel is entirely non-positive.
- `decodeMultiple()` — returns all local maxima above `PointDecoderParams::threshold`.

## Coordinate Scaling

When `DecoderContext::target_image_size` is non-zero, coordinates are scaled linearly from tensor
space to the original image size. When `target_image_size` is `{0, 0}`, coordinates are returned
in tensor pixel space.

## Validation

Both entry points validate at runtime:

- Tensor is defined and 4-dimensional
- `batch_index` and `source_channel` are in range
- `height` and `width` are positive and match the tensor spatial dimensions

Invalid inputs throw `std::invalid_argument` or `std::out_of_range` after logging the failure at
`spdlog::debug` level.
